preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 56% to lift this match on a neutral ground, leaving Portugal with a 17% win probability. That represents a substantial gap; Spain enters as clear favorite while Portugal faces steep odds of an outright victory.

The draw probability of 28% suggests competitive balance in the middle ground—neither team dominates the scenario space decisively. The 39-point spread between Spain's peak and Portugal's trough indicates meaningful separation in quality or capability, yet the draw's meaningful share implies this is not a mismatch. Roughly one in four outcomes sees neither side break through.

The market consensus prices Spain noticeably lower than the model does, at 50% versus 56%, and simultaneously assigns Portugal 6 percentage points higher at 24%. The sportsbook and Polymarket figures align closely with each other but diverge from the model estimate on the Spain win by around 5–6 points. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading of the match is correct.
